_description: Configuration for updating expired user emails in Alma

_required_settings._note: You must specify either set_id OR tsv_file (not both)

_set_id_note: Alma USER set ID - get this from Alma Configuration > Manage Sets
_tsv_file_note: Alternative to set_id: path to TSV file with user IDs (one per line)
_days_expired_note: Minimum days expired to qualify (730 = 2 years, 1095 = 3 years)
_dry_run_note: SAFETY: true = no changes made, false = actual updates. START WITH TRUE!

_optional_settings._note: These settings have reasonable defaults but can be customized

_max_users_note: Limit for testing (null = no limit, 10 = process only first 10 users)
_batch_size_note: Users processed per batch (for rate limiting)
_output_dir_note: Directory for log files and CSV results
_safety_checklist: 1. Set environment to SANDBOX for testing
2. Set dry_run to true for initial tests
3. Use max_users for small test runs first
6. Only set dry_run to false after successful testing
7. Only use PRODUCTION after SANDBOX testing
_workflow_steps: 1. Get USER set ID from Alma Configuration > Manage Sets
2. Update set_id and email_pattern in this config
6. When satisfied, set dry_run=false for live update
